tweets.text:
This is one of the weird paradoxes of building things today. You have to build things of consequence. 

The scale is different though. A 6-figures SWE wouldn't even work for you if it were some speculative thing. But for 5 figures in tokens, you can find out yourself, just you.

Oh this is probably an under-rated part of what is happening. You have to let the tokens rip. 

The magic happens because you use the frontier model and you have to be doing things that have economic value such that you can look back and say "It Does X" and it's worth it to you https://t.co/pndXprXaTT
Thanks Gus https://t.co/LYeOfaF61i https://t.co/1ypXfhUa2O

tweets.text:
OpenClaw 和Claude 产品发布竞争时间线：

1 月初：OpenClaw（当时叫 Clawdbot）爆火，Mac Mini 卖断货。

1 月 12 日：Anthropic 悄悄发布了 Cowork 研究预览版：一个桌面沙盒，Claude 可以在里面执行任务、管理文件、跟你协作。

1 月底：Anthropic 发了商标投诉。项目改名叫 OpenClaw，热度更高了。

终于1.5倍速听完了，原始有7个小时，很棒的访谈。

印象最深刻的是何恺明被提及n多次。

还有这两个顶级AI研究员的娱乐也是打魔兽世界和炉石传说 ，哈哈哈！ https://t.co/G0vdwiuFJ5

tweets.text: If the same person comes to you with problems everyday, and they're not your spouse or child, probably worth removing them from your life until they can come to you with solutions.

When you die, all your problems become other people's problems.
But when other people die, most of their problems die with them
Which means most of the things you call problems never really mattered to begin with
If you can't pass them on, they're probably not worth holding onto.
If your friends aren't happy about your success, they're not your friends.

x.name: Andrew Huberman
x.bio: Professor of Neurobiology and Ophthalmology at Stanford Medicine • Host of Huberman Lab • Focused on science and health research and public education

tweets.text: I was going to send this to @Brady_H but I think many people will enjoy this biography of legendary Oregon track and field coach and Nike cofounder Bill Bowerman. Unique teaching tactics (that would never ever fly today!) https://t.co/XbCUh5jESl

In all seriousness, the sunlight piece is probably the most underestimated in terms of its impact on health. It’s the easiest no matter where you live. It costs nothing. But people tend to resist it because it requires you meet the suns schedule. But oh so worth it.

And yes sunlight and sleep and nutrition and quality social connection (per your needs and wants) all vital too. First to say “what about sunlight and sleep etc” wins the “you have too much time on your hands” award and a MRI referral
